preamble: Deaffrication. The alveolar affricate [ts] simplifies to a simple alveolar stop [t], losing its fricative release. This is commonly seen in child language or in the adaptation of loanwords into languages that lack affricates.

examples.note: Loanwords into languages without affricates often see deaffrication.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
